response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile engineer with genuine geospatial/GIS depth (GISCollective, Green Map) and proven low-latency distributed-systems work (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic handling millions of requests/second with async I/O, fibers, and caching). However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and the candidate's C++ is dated (2014–2015 at National Instruments and a thesis project) with no Rust at all; their core stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang. The systems and geospatial background is relevant enough to warrant a conversation, but the language gap against the central requirement is significant.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS background building real map-data platforms
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed-systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching)
Solid engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, Linux, DevOps
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated; no Rust experience
GIS data collection differs from production routing-tile pipelines
Primary stack (TypeScript/Node/DLang) misaligned with the C++/Rust core
